skippedBundles.reason: No OpenAI API, model, retrieval or evaluation change.
No MCP protocol or tool contract change.
Airtable-backed mapping persistence was left in place but no Airtable API contract was changed.

implementationSummary.templateSelection: Use the first non-header sticky-note card under each category heading, preferring blank or placeholder cards.
implementationSummary.firstEntryAction: PATCH existing template sticky when it is still blank or placeholder text.
implementationSummary.laterEntryAction: POST new sticky-note below the latest synced item with the existing grid gap.
idempotency: Detect existing entries by journal-entry marker when D1 mapping annotations are available.
Detect existing entries by exact body/category match when live widgets lack mapping annotations.
Return already-synced outcomes with preserved: true.
tagHandling: Preserve existing template tags such as Raspberry category and Snowberry project tags.
Do not create category/project tags from ResearchOps as Mint tags.
Create only explicit ResearchOps user-created tags as Mint tags.
Strip the internal researchOpsUserTags hint before forwarding write requests to Mural.
Apply known Mural tag ids to the sticky after the sticky-note create or update response returns a widget id.
followUpCorrection: Treat exact wide category-label sticky-note widgets as headers so purple column labels are not patched as journal entries.
Write sticky content without unsupported tag fields, then apply board-known tag ids and newly-created Mint user tag ids to the resulting widget.
Address Codex review thread PRRT_kwDOP3Td2M6JDoxV by preventing one body-matched Mural widget from being reused for multiple distinct journal entry ids in status and hydrate passes.

Ignore decorative purple title/header cards when selecting reusable journal templates or existing entry cards; require white content-card geometry and allow filled white cards to anchor later placements when category header text is unreadable.

Repair polluted boards by refusing to count synced widgets above the resolved content-card row, then deleting stale synced widgets outside the content flow after the correct white card is updated or created.

Recognise manually-created white Mural cards as already synced when they are in the correct column and their body text matches a journal entry, even when they lack the internal journal-entry marker and Mural returns text in plainText, htmlText, content, properties or data fields.
Follow Mural widget-list pagination via the public API next cursor so status and hydrate scan all board widgets rather than only the first page.
Extract visible card text from nested Mural payloads such as { text: { plainText: '...' } } so object-shaped text fields are not normalised to [object Object].
uiReporting: Compact journal Mural sync status reports already-present entries as left unchanged.
Journal tabs sync panel reports already-present entries as left unchanged.

validationRun.summary: Includes duplicate-body entries, purple decorative-card, stale-widget repair, paginated widget loading, nested Mural text payloads and manual-card recognition regression coverage.

validationRun.command: node --check infra/cloudflare/src/service/mural-journal-sync-layout.js && node --check infra/cloudflare/src/service/mural-journal-sync-safe-tags.js && node --check tests/mural-journal-sync-safe-tags-runtime.test.js && node --check public/js/journal-mural-sync-compact.js && node --check public/js/journal-tabs.js

residualRisks: No live Mural board mutation was performed from the local Codex session.

The safe-tags wrapper applies existing Mural tags by id when the Mural tags list returns them. If a template carries a tag name that Mural does not report as an existing board tag, the wrapper omits that template tag from new cards rather than creating it as Mint.
